violations.text: Hash Browns on the cookline area were observed held at 118 degrees Fahrenheit. Per the PIC and Taco bell standards, hash browns were immediately voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2026

At the time of inspection, sauces/dressings were observed being held out of temperature control at the prep line area while using Time as a Public Health Control. The facility currently does not have approval by RIDOH Center for Food Protection for use of Time as a Public Health Control. PIC is to submit a request for approval of a Variance for Using Time as a Public Health Control for the sauces (Creamy Jalapeno, Creamy Chipotle, Spicy Ranch, Avocado Ranch) as soon as possible. RIDOH CFP Supervisor information supplied at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the (walk-in cooler fans shrouds) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2024

Cheese and sauce was observed held at 48-50 degrees Fahrenheit above the cold hold line. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was recently placed in unit. Food was moved to colder refrigeration to rapidly cool. Food must be stored below cold hold line. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2023
